name: Banshee
primary_sources: Irish Wonders by D. R. McAnally

distinctive_features: grey cloak over a green dress
sometimes dressed in white with red hair
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: short, between one foot and four feet tall
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: often seen crouched beneath trees or flying past in the moonlight

domain_specific_visual.hair_combing: seen combing her hair while keening
domain_specific_visual.shrouded_appearance: appears as a shrouded woman with a veiled face

functional_attributes.primary_function: heralds the death of a family member

domain_specific_functional.keening: wails a lament to signal death
domain_specific_functional.family_association: associated with specific ancient Irish families

relationships.description: Laments the descendants of ancient Irish families

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbolizes impending death and is a part of mourning traditions

contrastive_constraints: NOT a creature from non-Celtic folklore
